item.title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
item.description:
By Keivan Alizadeh, Parshin Shojaee, Minsik Cho, Mehrdad Farajtabar
Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM) have approached this challenge by agentic way of...

item.title: FinToolBench: Evaluating LLM Agents for Real-World Financial Tool Use
item.description:
By Jiaxuan Lu, Kong Wang, Yemin Wang, Qingmei Tang, Hongwei Zeng, et al.
The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into the financial domain is driving a paradigm shift from passive information retrieval to dynamic, agentic interaction. While general-purpose tool learning has witnessed a surge in benchmarks, the financial sector, characterized by high stakes, stric...

item.title: Thinking in Uncertainty: Mitigating Hallucinations in MLRMs with Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding
item.description:
By Zhongxing Xu, Zhonghua Wang, Zhe Qian, Dachuan Shi, Feilong Tang, et al.
Recent advancements in multimodal large reasoning models (MLRMs) have significantly improved performance in visual question answering. However, we observe that transition words (e.g., because, however, and wait) are closely associated with hallucinations and tend to exhibit high-entropy states. We a...

item.title: One-Eval: An Agentic System for Automated and Traceable LLM Evaluation
item.description:
By Chengyu Shen, Yanheng Hou, Minghui Pan, Runming He, Zhen Hao Wong, et al.
Reliable evaluation is essential for developing and deploying large language models, yet in practice it often requires substantial manual effort: practitioners must identify appropriate benchmarks, reproduce heterogeneous evaluation codebases, configure dataset schema mappings, and interpret aggrega...

item.title: Test-Time Strategies for More Efficient and Accurate Agentic RAG
item.description:
By Brian Zhang, Deepti Guntur, Zhiyang Zuo, Abhinav Sharma, Shreyas Chaudhari, et al.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems face challenges with complex, multihop questions, and agentic frameworks such as Search-R1 (Jin et al., 2025), which operates iteratively, have been proposed to address these complexities. However, such approaches can introduce inefficiencies, including r...

item.title: VoXtream2: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking rate control
item.description:
By Nikita Torgashov, Gustav Eje Henter, Gabriel Skantze
Full-stream text-to-speech (TTS) for interactive systems must start speaking with minimal delay while remaining controllable as text arrives incrementally. We present VoXtream2, a zero-shot full-stream TTS model with dynamic speaking-rate control that can be updated mid-utterance on the fly. VoXtrea...

item.title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Environments and Evaluations for Stateful Agentic Planning and Tool Use in Enterprise Settings
item.description:
By Shiva Krishna Reddy Malay, Shravan Nayak, Jishnu Sethumadhavan Nair, Sagar Davasam, Aman Tiwari, et al.
Large language models are shifting from passive information providers to active agents intended for complex workflows. However, their deployment as reliable AI workers in enterprise is stalled by benchmarks that fail to capture the intricacies of professional environments, specifically, the need for...

item.title: Measuring Primitive Accumulation: An Information-Theoretic Approach to Capitalist Enclosure in PIK2, Indonesia
item.description:
By Sandy Hardian Susanto Herho, Alfita Puspa Handayani, Karina Aprilia Sujatmiko, Faruq Khadami, Iwan Pramesti Anwar
Large-scale land enclosure for speculative mega-development constitutes a non-equilibrium spatial process whose velocity, topology, and irreversibility remain poorly quantified. We study the Pantai Indah Kapuk 2 (PIK2) coastal mega-development north of Jakarta, Indonesia, using eight years (2017--20...

item.title: sebis at ArchEHR-QA 2026: How Much Can You Do Locally? Evaluating Grounded EHR QA on a Single Notebook
item.description:
By Ibrahim Ebrar Yurt, Fabian Karl, Tejaswi Choppa, Florian Matthes
Clinical question answering over electronic health records (EHRs) can help clinicians and patients access relevant medical information more efficiently. However, many recent approaches rely on large cloud-based models, which are difficult to deploy in clinical environments due to privacy constraints...

item.title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
item.description:
By Anbang Wang, Yuzhuo Ao, Shangzhe Wu, Chi-Keung Tang
Native 3D generative models have achieved remarkable fidelity and speed, yet they suffer from a critical limitation: inability to prescribe precise structural articulations, where precise structural control within the native 3D space remains underexplored. This paper proposes SK-Adapter, a simple an...

item.title: Garments2Look: A Multi-Reference Dataset for High-Fidelity Outfit-Level Virtual Try-On with Clothing and Accessories
item.description:
By Junyao Hu, Zhongwei Cheng, Waikeung Wong, Xingxing Zou
Virtual try-on (VTON) has advanced single-garment visualization, yet real-world fashion centers on full outfits with multiple garments, accessories, fine-grained categories, layering, and diverse styling, remaining beyond current VTON systems. Existing datasets are category-limited and lack outfit d...

item.title: Autonomous Agents Coordinating Distributed Discovery Through Emergent Artifact Exchange
item.description:
By Fiona Y. Wang, Lee Marom, Subhadeep Pal, Rachel K. Luu, Wei Lu, et al.
We present ScienceClaw + Infinite, a framework for autonomous scientific investigation in which independent agents conduct research without central coordination, and any contributor can deploy new agents into a shared ecosystem. The system is built around three components: an extensible registry of ...

item.title: OxyGen: Unified KV Cache Management for Vision-Language-Action Models under Multi-Task Parallelism
item.description:
By Xiangyu Li, Huaizhi Tang, Xin Ding, Weijun Wang, Ting Cao, et al.
Embodied AI agents increasingly require parallel execution of multiple tasks, such as manipulation, conversation, and memory construction, from shared observations under distinct time constraints. Recent Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) architecturally support such ...

item.title: AgentProcessBench: Diagnosing Step-Level Process Quality in Tool-Using Agents
item.description:
By Shengda Fan, Xuyan Ye, Yupeng Huo, Zhi-Yuan Chen, Yiju Guo, et al.
While Large Language Models (LLMs) have evolved into tool-using agents, they remain brittle in long-horizon interactions. Unlike mathematical reasoning where errors are often rectifiable via backtracking, tool-use failures frequently induce irreversible side effects, making accurate step-level verif...

item.title: Make it SING: Analyzing Semantic Invariants in Classifiers
item.description:
By Harel Yadid, Meir Yossef Levi, Roy Betser, Guy Gilboa
All classifiers, including state-of-the-art vision models, possess invariants, partially rooted in the geometry of their linear mappings. These invariants, which reside in the null-space of the classifier, induce equivalent sets of inputs that map to identical outputs. The semantic content of these ...

item.title: Spectrum Matching: a Unified Perspective for Superior Diffusability in Latent Diffusion
item.description:
By Mang Ning, Mingxiao Li, Le Zhang, Lanmiao Liu, Matthew B. Blaschko, et al.
In this paper, we study the diffusability (learnability) of variational autoencoders (VAE) in latent diffusion. First, we show that pixel-space diffusion trained with an MSE objective is inherently biased toward learning low and mid spatial frequencies, and that the power-law power spectral density ...

item.title: VisionCoach: Reinforcing Grounded Video Reasoning via Visual-Perception Prompting
item.description:
By Daeun Lee, Shoubin Yu, Yue Zhang, Mohit Bansal
Video reasoning requires models to locate and track question-relevant evidence across frames. While reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards improves accuracy, it still struggles to achieve reliable spatio-temporal grounding during the reasoning process. Moreover, improving grounding typi...

item.title: POLCA: Stochastic Generative Optimization with LLM
item.description:
By Xuanfei Ren, Allen Nie, Tengyang Xie, Ching-An Cheng
Optimizing complex systems, ranging from LLM prompts to multi-turn agents, traditionally requires labor-intensive manual iteration. We formalize this challenge as a stochastic generative optimization problem where a generative language model acts as the optimizer, guided by numerical rewards and tex...

item.title: RS-WorldModel: a Unified Model for Remote Sensing Understanding and Future Sense Forecasting
item.description:
By Linrui Xu, Zhongan Wang, Fei Shen, Gang Xu, Huiping Zhuang, et al.
Remote sensing world models aim to both explain observed changes and forecast plausible futures, two tasks that share spatiotemporal priors. Existing methods, however, typically address them separately, limiting cross-task transfer. We present RS-WorldModel, a unified world model for remote sensing ...

item.title: Riemannian Motion Generation: A Unified Framework for Human Motion Representation and Generation via Riemannian Flow Matching
item.description:
By Fangran Miao, Jian Huang, Ting Li
Human motion generation is often learned in Euclidean spaces, although valid motions follow structured non-Euclidean geometry. We present Riemannian Motion Generation (RMG), a unified framework that represents motion on a product manifold and learns dynamics via Riemannian flow matching. RMG factori...

item.title: CCTU: A Benchmark for Tool Use under Complex Constraints
item.description:
By Junjie Ye, Guoqiang Zhang, Wenjie Fu, Tao Gui, Qi Zhang, et al.
Solving problems through tool use under explicit constraints constitutes a highly challenging yet unavoidable scenario for large language models (LLMs), requiring capabilities such as function calling, instruction following, and self-refinement. However, progress has been hindered by the absence of ...

item.title: When Does Sparsity Mitigate the Curse of Depth in LLMs
item.description:
By Dilxat Muhtar, Xinyuan Song, Sebastian Pokutta, Max Zimmer, Nico Pelleriti, et al.
Recent work has demonstrated the curse of depth in large language models (LLMs), where later layers contribute less to learning and representation than earlier layers. Such under-utilization is linked to the accumulated growth of variance in Pre-Layer Normalization, which can push deep blocks toward...

item.title: Anatomy of a Lie: A Multi-Stage Diagnostic Framework for Tracing Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
item.description:
By Lexiang Xiong, Qi Li, Jingwen Ye, Xinchao Wang
Vision-Language Models (VLMs) frequently "hallucinate" - generate plausible yet factually incorrect statements - posing a critical barrier to their trustworthy deployment. In this work, we propose a new paradigm for diagnosing hallucinations, recasting them from static output errors into dynamic pat...

item.title: The PokeAgent Challenge: Competitive and Long-Context Learning at Scale
item.description:
By Seth Karten, Jake Grigsby, Tersoo Upaa, Junik Bae, Seonghun Hong, et al.
We present the PokeAgent Challenge, a large-scale benchmark for decision-making research built on Pokemon's multi-agent battle system and expansive role-playing game (RPG) environment. Partial observability, game-theoretic reasoning, and long-horizon planning remain open problems for frontier AI, ye...

item.title: Effective Distillation to Hybrid xLSTM Architectures
item.description:
By Lukas Hauzenberger, Niklas Schmidinger, Thomas Schmied, Anamaria-Roberta Hartl, David Stap, et al.
There have been numerous attempts to distill quadratic attention-based large language models (LLMs) into sub-quadratic linearized architectures. However, despite extensive research, such distilled models often fail to match the performance of their teacher LLMs on various downstream tasks. We set ou...

item.title: From Passive Observer to Active Critic: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Process Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation
item.description:
By Yibin Liu, Yaxing Lyu, Daqi Gao, Zhixuan Liang, Weiliang Tang, et al.
Accurate process supervision remains a critical challenge for long-horizon robotic manipulation. A primary bottleneck is that current video MLLMs, trained primarily under a Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) paradigm, function as passive "Observers" that recognize ongoing events rather than evaluating the...

item.title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
item.description:
By MiroMind Team, S. Bai, L. Bing, L. Lei, R. Li, et al.
We present MiroThinker-1.7, a new research agent designed for complex long-horizon reasoning tasks. Building on this foundation, we further introduce MiroThinker-H1, which extends the agent with heavy-duty reasoning capabilities for more reliable multi-step problem solving. In particular, MiroThinke...

item.title: Tri-Prompting: Video Diffusion with Unified Control over Scene, Subject, and Motion
item.description:
By Zhenghong Zhou, Xiaohang Zhan, Zhiqin Chen, Soo Ye Kim, Nanxuan Zhao, et al.
Recent video diffusion models have made remarkable strides in visual quality, yet precise, fine-grained control remains a key bottleneck that limits practical customizability for content creation. For AI video creators, three forms of control are crucial: (i) scene composition, (ii) multi-view consi...

item.title: HorizonMath: Measuring AI Progress Toward Mathematical Discovery with Automatic Verification
item.description:
By Erik Y. Wang, Sumeet Motwani, James V. Roggeveen, Eliot Hodges, Dulhan Jayalath, et al.
Can AI make progress on important, unsolved mathematical problems? Large language models are now capable of sophisticated mathematical and scientific reasoning, but whether they can perform novel research is still widely debated and underexplored. We introduce HorizonMath, a benchmark of over 100 pr...

item.title: Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments
item.description:
By Heng Fang, Shangru Li, Shuhan Wang, Xuanyang Xi, Dingkang Liang, et al.
Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models excel in static manipulation but struggle in dynamic environments with moving targets. This performance gap primarily stems from a scarcity of dynamic manipulation datasets and the reliance of mainstream VLAs on single-frame observations, restricting their spatiot...

item.title: FlashSampling: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Sampling
item.description:
By Tomas Ruiz, Zhen Qin, Yifan Zhang, Xuyang Shen, Yiran Zhong, et al.
Sampling from a categorical distribution is mathematically simple, but in large-vocabulary decoding, it often triggers extra memory traffic and extra kernels after the LM head. We present FlashSampling, an exact sampling primitive that fuses sampling into the LM-head matmul and never materializes th...

item.title: Semi-Autonomous Formalization of the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau Equilibrium
item.description:
By Vasily Ilin
We present a complete Lean 4 formalization of the equilibrium characterization in the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau (VML) system, which describes the motion of charged plasma. The project demonstrates the full AI-assisted mathematical research loop: an AI reasoning model (Gemini DeepThink) generated the pro...

item.title: ViT-AdaLA: Adapting Vision Transformers with Linear Attention
item.description:
By Yifan Li, Seunghyun Yoon, Viet Dac Lai, Franck Dernoncourt, Jason Kuen, et al.
Vision Transformers (ViTs) based vision foundation models (VFMs) have achieved remarkable performance across diverse vision tasks, but suffer from quadratic complexity that limits scalability to long sequences. Existing linear attention approaches for ViTs are typically trained from scratch, requiri...

item.title: OneWorld: Taming Scene Generation with 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder
item.description:
By Sensen Gao, Zhaoqing Wang, Qihang Cao, Dongdong Yu, Changhu Wang, et al.
Existing diffusion-based 3D scene generation methods primarily operate in 2D image/video latent spaces, which makes maintaining cross-view appearance and geometric consistency inherently challenging. To bridge this gap, we present OneWorld, a framework that performs diffusion directly within a coher...

item.title: Rethinking UMM Visual Generation: Masked Modeling for Efficient Image-Only Pre-training
item.description:
By Peng Sun, Jun Xie, Tao Lin
Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) are often constrained by the pre-training of their visual generation components, which typically relies on inefficient paradigms and scarce, high-quality text-image paired data. In this paper, we systematically analyze pre-training recipes for UMM visual generation a...

item.title: Polyglot-Lion: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore via Balanced Fine-Tuning of Qwen3-ASR
item.description:
By Quy-Anh Dang, Chris Ngo
We present Polyglot-Lion, a family of compact multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) models tailored for the linguistic landscape of Singapore, covering English, Mandarin, Tamil, and Malay. Our models are obtained by fine-tuning Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and Qwen3-ASR-1.7B exclusively on publicly avai...

item.title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
By Haomin Wang, Qi Wei, Qianli Ma, Shengyuan Ding, Jinhui Yin, et al.
With the rapid advancement of vision-language models, an increasing number of studies have explored their potential for SVG generation tasks. Although existing approaches improve performance by constructing large-scale SVG datasets and introducing SVG-specific tokens, they still suffer from limited ...

item.title: Learning Human-Object Interaction for 3D Human Pose Estimation from LiDAR Point Clouds
item.description:
By Daniel Sungho Jung, Dohee Cho, Kyoung Mu Lee
Understanding humans from LiDAR point clouds is one of the most critical tasks in autonomous driving due to its close relationships with pedestrian safety, yet it remains challenging in the presence of diverse human-object interactions and cluttered backgrounds. Nevertheless, existing methods largel...

item.title: TRUST-SQL: Tool-Integrated Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-SQL over Unknown Schemas
item.description:
By Ai Jian, Xiaoyun Zhang, Wanrou Du, Jingqing Ruan, Jiangbo Pei, et al.
Text-to-SQL parsing has achieved remarkable progress under the Full Schema Assumption. However, this premise fails in real-world enterprise environments where databases contain hundreds of tables with massive noisy metadata. Rather than injecting the full schema upfront, an agent must actively ident...

item.title: Kinema4D: Kinematic 4D World Modeling for Spatiotemporal Embodied Simulation
item.description:
By Mutian Xu, Tianbao Zhang, Tianqi Liu, Zhaoxi Chen, Xiaoguang Han, et al.
Simulating robot-world interactions is a cornerstone of Embodied AI. Recently, a few works have shown promise in leveraging video generations to transcend the rigid visual/physical constraints of traditional simulators. However, they primarily operate in 2D space or are guided by static environmenta...

item.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents
item.description:
By Yongyuan Liang, Shijie Zhou, Yu Gu, Hao Tan, Gang Wu, et al.
Recent advances in multimodal agents have improved computer-use interaction and tool-usage, yet most existing systems remain reactive, optimizing actions in isolation without reasoning about future states or long-term goals. This limits planning coherence and prevents agents from reliably solving hi...

item.title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios
item.description:
By Jian Yang, Wei Zhang, Jiajun Wu, Junhang Cheng, Shawn Guo, et al.
Recent code large language models have achieved remarkable progress on general programming tasks. Nevertheless, their performance degrades significantly in industrial scenarios that require reasoning about hardware semantics, specialized language constructs, and strict resource constraints. To addre...

item.title: M^3: Dense Matching Meets Multi-View Foundation Models for Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM
item.description:
By Kerui Ren, Guanghao Li, Changjian Jiang, Yingxiang Xu, Tao Lu, et al.
Streaming reconstruction from uncalibrated monocular video remains challenging, as it requires both high-precision pose estimation and computationally efficient online refinement in dynamic environments. While coupling 3D foundation models with SLAM frameworks is a promising paradigm, a critical bot...

item.title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models
item.description:
By Tianzhu Ye, Li Dong, Qingxiu Dong, Xun Wu, Shaohan Huang, et al.
The prevailing paradigm for improving large language models relies on offline training with human annotations or simulated environments, leaving the rich experience accumulated during real-world deployment entirely unexploited. We propose Online Experiential Learning (OEL), a framework that enables ...

item.title: SocialOmni: Benchmarking Audio-Visual Social Interactivity in Omni Models
item.description:
By Tianyu Xie, Jinfa Huang, Yuexiao Ma, Rongfang Luo, Yan Yang, et al.
Omni-modal large language models (OLMs) redefine human-machine interaction by natively integrating audio, vision, and text. However, existing OLM benchmarks remain anchored to static, accuracy-centric tasks, leaving a critical gap in assessing social interactivity, the fundamental capacity to naviga...

item.title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation
item.description:
By Abhay Deshpande, Maya Guru, Rose Hendrix, Snehal Jauhri, Ainaz Eftekhar, et al.
A prevailing view in robot learning is that simulation alone is not enough; effective sim-to-real transfer is widely believed to require at least some real-world data collection or task-specific fine-tuning to bridge the gap between simulated and physical environments. We challenge that assumption. ...

item.title: SegviGen: Repurposing 3D Generative Model for Part Segmentation
item.description:
By Lin Li, Haoran Feng, Zehuan Huang, Haohua Chen, Wenbo Nie, et al.
We introduce SegviGen, a framework that repurposes native 3D generative models for 3D part segmentation. Existing pipelines either lift strong 2D priors into 3D via distillation or multi-view mask aggregation, often suffering from cross-view inconsistency and blurred boundaries, or explore native 3D...

item.title: Efficient Reasoning on the Edge
item.description:
By Yelysei Bondarenko, Thomas Hehn, Rob Hesselink, Romain Lepert, Fabio Valerio Massoli, et al.
Large language models (LLMs) with chain-of-thought reasoning achieve state-of-the-art performance across complex problem-solving tasks, but their verbose reasoning traces and large context requirements make them impractical for edge deployment. These challenges include high token generation costs, l...

item.title: Demystifing Video Reasoning
item.description:
By Ruisi Wang, Zhongang Cai, Fanyi Pu, Junxiang Xu, Wanqi Yin, et al.
Recent advances in video generation have revealed an unexpected phenomenon: diffusion-based video models exhibit non-trivial reasoning capabilities. Prior work attributes this to a Chain-of-Frames (CoF) mechanism, where reasoning is assumed to unfold sequentially across video frames. In this work, w...

item.title: WorldCam: Interactive Autoregressive 3D Gaming Worlds with Camera Pose as a Unifying Geometric Representation
item.description:
By Jisu Nam, Yicong Hong, Chun-Hao Paul Huang, Feng Liu, JoungBin Lee, et al.
Recent advances in video diffusion transformers have enabled interactive gaming world models that allow users to explore generated environments over extended horizons. However, existing approaches struggle with precise action control and long-horizon 3D consistency. Most prior works treat user actio...
